canonical_definition: Based on the provided data from a single paper, factual grounding is a construct defined as the degree to which a model's outputs align with real-world distributions and factual information. The source presents it as a goal of model alignment, seeking to "guide models toward producing outputs grounded in factual information and real-world context." This construct is operationalized conceptually through the objective of aligning a model with a "real-world" distribution. This approach is explicitly contrasted with aligning to an "equal" distribution, which the paper associates with social equity. This contrast highlights a stated relationship between factual grounding and the concept of fairness.
papers: CREPE: Rapid ChestX-ray Report Evaluation by Predicting Multi-category Error Counts
Concept-pedia: a Wide-coverage Semantically-annotated Multimodal Dataset
Enhancing Efficiency and Exploration in Reinforcement Learning forLLMs
HyperKGR: Knowledge Graph Reasoning in Hyperbolic Space with Graph Neural Network Encoding Symbolic Path
OMS: On-the-fly, Multi-Objective, Self-Reflective Ad Keyword Generation viaLLMAgent
UNCLE: Benchmarking Uncertainty Expressions in Long-Form Generation

definitions.text: The degree to which a model's outputs are aligned with real-world distributions and factual information.
definitions.paper: Enhancing Efficiency and Exploration in Reinforcement Learning forLLMs

relationships.paper: Enhancing Efficiency and Exploration in Reinforcement Learning forLLMs

emnlp25: Enhancing Efficiency and Exploration in Reinforcement Learning forLLMs
